---
title: Better science communication with<br><quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> apps
subtitle: <em>With <rosa>remote sensing</rosa> examples</em> | <gris>2026-04-01</gris>
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#224289"
author:
  - name: <vhg>Víctor Gauto, **MS** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9960-8558)</vhg>
    affiliation:
      - name: <rosa><b>GISTAQ</b> (UTN-FRRe)</rosa>
        url: https://www.instagram.com/gistaq.utn/
      - name: <rosa><b>IIDTHH</b> (UNNE, CONICET)</rosa>
        url: https://iidthh.conicet.gov.ar/
      - name: <rosa><b>Instituto Gulich</b> (UNC, CONAE)<rosa>
        url: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/
    email: victor.gauto@ca.free.utn.edu.ar
    corresponding: true
  - name: Matías<br>Bonansea, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1953-2595)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>ICBIA</b> (UNRC, CONICET)
        url: https://icbia.conicet.gov.ar/
  - name: Anabella<br>Ferral, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9383-7728)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>Instituto Gulich</b> (UNC, CONAE)
        url: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/
  - name: Osvaldo<br>Cardozo, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0345-4505)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>IIDTHH</b> (UNNE, CONICET)
        url: https://iidthh.conicet.gov.ar/
  - name: Claudia<br>Giardino, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3937-4988)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>IREA</b> (CNR)
        url: http://www.irea.cnr.it/
format:
  revealjs:
    fig-width: 7
    fig-height: 7
    fig-dpi: 300
    code-link: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-copy: true
    code-fold: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-summary: Show code
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    auto-play-media: true
    highlight-style: kate
    width: 1200
    incremental: false
    reference-location: document
    transition: none
    logo: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/wp-content/uploads/sites/68/2022/04/G-CLARO-C.png
    footer: |
      <rosa>Mgtr. <blanco>Víctor Gauto</blanco> <span class="line-md--cog-filled-loop"></span> Better science communication with <blanco>shiny</blanco> apps
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    hash-type: title
    # date: 2026-04-01
    center-title-slide: false
    navigation-mode: linear
    link-external-icon: false
    link-external-newwindow: true
    output-location: column
    theme:
      - extras/mi_estilo.scss
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel='shortcut icon' href='img/favicon.jpg'/>
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
  warning: false
lightbox: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
filters: 
  - roughnotation
---

# Outline {.center .unnumbered}

<!-- {.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} -->

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column  width="45%"}

- Introduction [$\S$ @sec-introduction]
- Motivation [$\S$ @sec-motivation]
- When to develop a <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> app? [$\S$ @sec-when]
- Reactive programming [$\S$ @sec-reactive]
- Why use <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2>? [$\S$ @sec-why]
- Conclusion [$\S$ @sec-conclusion]
- Resources [$\S$ @sec-resources]

:::

::: {.column  width="55%"}

<center>Slides available online</center>

::: {.r-fit-text}

```{.r}
https://vhgauto.github.io/seminario4-gulich-slides/
```

![](img/qr_seminario.svg){width="70%" fig-align="center"}

:::

:::

::::

# Introduction {#sec-introduction .center}

- Apps are [everywhere]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, but not always associated with data science communication.
- Using apps, the user is compelled to [explore]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} and [play]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} with the available options, changing the outputs at will.
- A good app can trigger [curiosity]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} and allows our audience to ask new questions.

<center>
{{< iconify fluent:apps-48-filled size=8x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}
</center>

# Motivation {#sec-motivation .center}

Once a **Research Project** is finished, we proceed to [communicate]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} the primary results.

- <u>Academic environment</u>: papers, congress, posters, conferences.
- Web site, social network, news outlet.

Usually, to capture the attention's audience we apply some basic rules:

- Short, impactful [titles]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.
- Good and simple [visualizations]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.
- A proper [narrative]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} to explain complex ideas.

As an alternative, we can change the audience approach: from a [passive]{.rn-fragment rn-type=crossed-off rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} perspective, to an [active]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} and more engaging perspective.

# When to develop a <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> app? {#sec-when .center} 

Common contexts were one can rely on <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> to develop an app.

## Data exploration

A new dataset requires data [exploration]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, that usually initiate with statistical calculations, relationships between variables, plotting and modelling.

In a traditional way, this involves a lot of [iteration]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, to check multiple potentially uninteresting results and modify observations ranges.

To go back and forth requires a lot of typing and it's prone to [errors]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.

Custom functions can help to ease the recurrent task, but still requires a development environment, with a lot of [coding background]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.

<center>
{{< iconify material-symbols:data-exploration size=4x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify octicon:graph-16 size=4x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify carbon:box-plot size=4x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify carbon:qq-plot size=4x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}
</center>

An app it's an alternative for data exploration that can help you find [insight]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}. 

Apps can be [shared]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} allowing data exploration from all kinds of experts.

## Science communication

It's important to include proper [explanations]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} that follows along the **reactive** aspects of the app.

App features may include color palette selection, the capability to export some results, variable selections for plotting, etc. Simple features can improve the [understanding]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} of the results found.

<center>
{{< iconify material-symbols-light:communication-rounded size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify icon-park-twotone:communication size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify fluent:people-community-28-filled size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}
</center>

We can include links, videos, figures and other [resources]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} to complement what we want to communicate.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false} 

[Since an app allows the audience to change multiple variables, ranges, correlations, models, options, it's relevant to [not confuse]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, and that all reactive parts of the app are inline with the results we want to share.]{style='font-size: 1.3em;'}

:::

## Remote sensing example

- [Interactive maps]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} can be displayed by selecting different algorithms/parameters.
- [RGB maps]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} can be changed (illumination, contrast, gamma) to improve them.
- Plot [spectral signature]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, selecting dates, sites, atmospheric correction processor.
- [Date selection]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} for map inspection.
- [Color palette]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} settings for a personal touch.
- [Export]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} custom maps, filtered data and plots.
- Create [reports]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} of selected variables, sites and dates as a pdf file.
- Display main parameters values, for an easy visualization, as a [dashboard]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.
- Include [resources]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} as documentation, links and formatted bibliography.

## Paraná Project {.unnumbered}

::: {.r-fit-text}

```{.r}
https://vhgauto-seminario4-gulich.share.connect.posit.cloud
```

![](img/qr_seminario.svg){width="40%" fig-align="center"}

:::

# Why use <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada>? {#sec-why .center}

- <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> is a [tool]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} for app development, with {{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project >}} and {{< iconify akar-icons:python-fill >}} integration.
- Since a lot of work is done by coding processing, <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> offers a [known framework]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}.
- With [minimal]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, configuration a proper app is achieved.
- Multiple [publishing]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} alternatives are available, some of them for free.
- <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> allows to style the app as we like, since we can include [web development]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} tools.
- [Resources]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} available online and package documentation with books and plenty of examples.

## <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> extensions

::::: {.columns layout="[6,6]"}

:::: {.column}
- Theming

[bslib](https://rstudio.github.io/bslib/) provides a modern UI toolkit.

[fresh](https://dreamrs.github.io/fresh/) create a theme to personalize.

[linne](https://linne.john-coene.com/) makes basic CSS easier.

[gfonts](https://github.com/dreamRs/gfonts) offline Google Fonts.
::::

:::: {.column}
- User interface

[bsicons](https://github.com/rstudio/bsicons) bootstrap icons.

[viewR](https://itsmdivakaran.github.io/viewR/) advanced interactive data table.

[shinychat](https://posit-dev.github.io/shinychat/) LLM-powered chat interfaces.
::::

:::: {.column}
- Visualization

[vchartr](https://dreamrs.github.io/vchartr/index.html) for interactive charts.

[tsibbletalk](https://github.com/earowang/tsibbletalk) for exploring temporal patterns.

[g6R](https://cynkra.github.io/g6R/) allows to create interactive network.
::::

:::: {.column}
- Developer tools

[shinyuieditor](https://rstudio.github.io/shinyuieditor/) build application UIs by dragging-and-dropping.

[tidymodules](https://opensource.nibr.com/tidymodules/) an object-oriented approach to modules.

[shinytest2](https://rstudio.github.io/shinytest2/) testing Shiny applications.
::::

:::::

## <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> alternatives

{{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project width=80px >}}:

<center>
:::: {.columns layout="[4, 4, 4]" layout-valign="bottom"}

::: {.column}
![](img/plumber.svg){width="100"}

[plumber](https://www.rplumber.io/index.html)
:::

::: {.column}
![](img/flexdashboard.png){width="100"}

[flexdashboard](https://rstudio.github.io/flexdashboard/)
:::

::: {.column}
[crosstalk](https://rstudio.github.io/crosstalk/index.html)
:::

::::
</center>

{{< iconify akar-icons:python-fill width=80px >}}:

<center>
:::: {.columns layout="[4, 4, 4]" layout-valign="bottom"}

::: {.column}
{{< iconify devicon:streamlit width=100px >}}

[streamlit](https://streamlit.io/)
:::

::: {.column}
{{< iconify devicon:plotly width=100px >}}

[dash](https://plotly.com/dash/)
:::

::: {.column}
![](img/marimo.svg){width="100"}

[marimo](https://marimo.io/)
:::

::::
</center>

# Reactive programming {#sec-reactive .center}

As app users, we interact with [widgets]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, interface elements, such as sliders, numeric/text/date input, check box, file browser, etc.

When we work with data, we code in a direct way, so the functions are evaluated [immediately]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} and an output is generated at the spot.

But, in a **reactive** environment, we have to think in potential users and the many [request]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} that they can do, from the offer that we provide.

<center>
{{< iconify boxicons:slider size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify subway:calendar size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify icon-park-solid:list-checkbox size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify radix-icons:input size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}
</center>

An app is divided in two main parts: the [user interface]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, in the audience side, with the possibility to request calculations and the ability to configure settings. And the processing side, the [server]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3}, where all the processes are executed accordingly.

## Challenges

:::: {.columns layout=[8,4]}

::: {.column}

Since the app is made of an user interface and a server:

- We must think in all the [potential stages]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} of variables

- The [execution time]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} is a major aspect in the whole app, to offer a seamless user experience.

<center>
{{< iconify boxicons:workflow-alt size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify ri:function-add-fill size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify material-symbols:settings size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}} $\qquad$ {{< iconify svg-spinners:6-dots-scale-middle size=3x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}
</center>

- [Reproducibility]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} is a main concern, to ensure good results.
- The package [dependencies]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} can affect the deployment time and app loading.

:::

::: {.column}

![](img/Grabación de pantalla 2026-06-22 114700.mp4){loop="true"} 

:::

::::

## Deployment options

- [Connect Cloud](https://connect.posit.cloud/), from [Posit](https://posit.co/), offers plans for individuals and organizations, [free]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} and paid. Integrated with [Positron {{< iconify devicon-plain:positron >}}](https://positron.posit.co/) IDE.

- App embedded in a [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) web site deployed as a [static page]{.rn-fragment rn-type=underline rn-color=#F79CD4CC rn-strokeWidth=3} in [GitHub Pages](https://docs.github.com/es/pages) developed using [shinylive](https://opensource.posit.co/blog/2022-10-25_shinylive-extension/) for [{{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project >}}](https://posit-dev.github.io/r-shinylive/) and [{{< iconify akar-icons:python-fill >}}](https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html).

- [Amazon Web Services {{< iconify mdi:aws >}}](https://aws.amazon.com/es/blogs/architecture/scaling-rstudio-shiny-using-serverless-architecture-and-aws-fargate/) offers a scalable, secure, and serverless architecture.

- App transformed as a [package](https://mjfrigaard.github.io/shiny-app-pkgs/), a collections of pre-built, self-contained code, data and documentation.

- [golem](https://thinkr-open.github.io/golem/) for production-grade <quarto-portada2>shiny</quarto-portada2> applications.

# Conclusion {#sec-conclusion .center}

- An alternative for science communication is a reactive app.
- The audience can interact and request operations from the app, helping them to understands our results.
- The engagement from an app allows an active participation from the audience.
- Several scenarios can be potentially improve by apps, such as data exploration, in particular, remote sensing applications.
- High importance in reproducibility and performance.

# Resources {#sec-resources .smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} Mastering Shiny](https://mastering-shiny.org/)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} Engineering Production-Grade Shiny Apps](https://engineering-shiny.org/index.html)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} Outstanding User Interfaces with Shiny](https://unleash-shiny.rinterface.com/)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} The Shiny AWS Book](https://business-science.github.io/shiny-production-with-aws-book/)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} Shiny App-Packages](https://mjfrigaard.github.io/shiny-app-pkgs/)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} JavaScript for R](https://book.javascript-for-r.com/)

---

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} Mapping wildfires data in R with mapsf](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_BN7xOtbVAc)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} Making maps in R using TMAP](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QcR9s3wF5lY)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} How to FULLY Customize Leaflet Maps using R programming](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U0aJeaCnMeE)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} 3D digital elevation maps with elevatr, rayshader and rayvista in R](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kGadI6_ZIR4)

::: {.callout style='font-size: 2em;'}
Presentation made with {{< quarto >}} & {{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project style="color:#74aadb;" >}}

[Code available at {{< iconify mdi:github >}}](https://github.com/vhgauto/seminario4-gulich-slides)
:::

:::

::: {.column}

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} mapgl, R interface to Mapbox GL JS v3 and Maplibre GL JS](https://walker-data.com/mapgl/)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} elevatr, An R package for accessing elevation data](https://github.com/USEPA/elevatr)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} marmap, Import, Plot and Analyze Bathymetric and Topographic Data](https://github.com/ericpante/marmap)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} maptiles, Download, compose and display map tiles with R](https://github.com/riatelab/maptiles)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} geodata, download geographic data](https://rspatial.github.io/geodata/)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} rayvista, An R plugin for {rayshader} to view a 3D vista anywhere on earth](https://github.com/h-a-graham/rayvista)

---

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} Awesome Shiny Extensions](https://github.com/nanxstats/awesome-shiny-extensions)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} Shiny Developer Series](https://shinydevseries.com/)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} State of the (open source) web map](https://tim-salabim.codeberg.page/sotwm/sotwm.html)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} Tutorial: Adding Open Street Map Data to Rayshader Maps in R](https://www.tylermw.com/posts/data_visualization/adding-open-street-map-data-to-rayshader-maps-in-r.html)

:::

::::

# {.unnumbered background-color="#f79cd4"}

::: {style="text-align: center;"}

<gracias>Thanks for your time</gracias>

:::

<br>

:::: {.columns layout-ncol=2}

::: {.column style="text-align: center; font-size: 1em;"}

**MS Víctor Gauto**<br>[`victor.gauto@ca.frre.utn.edu.ar`](mailto:victor.gauto@ca.frre.utn.edu.ar)

:::

::: {.column layout-nrow=1 style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em;"}

[<blanco>{{< iconify ic:baseline-home >}}</blanco>](https://vhgauto.github.io/)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:twitter >}}</blanco>](https://twitter.com/vhgauto)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:instagram >}}</blanco>](https://www.instagram.com/vhgauto/)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:github >}}</blanco>](https://github.com/vhgauto)

[<blanco>{{< iconify ri:bluesky-fill >}}</blanco>](https://bsky.app/profile/vhgauto.bsky.social)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:mastodon >}}</blanco>](https://fosstodon.org/@vhgauto)

`@vhgauto`

:::

::::

::: {layout-nrow=1 layout-valign="center"}

<center>

![](img/Gistaq_sinfondo.png){width=165}

![](img/logo_ig3.png){width=165}

![](img/iidthh.png){width=165}

![](img/Conicet_Logo_con_letras.png){width=165}

</center>

:::

::: {layout-nrow=1 layout-valign="center"}

<center>

![](img/logo_utn6.png){width=165}

![](img/unne.png){width=165}

![](img/UNC_03_NEGRO.svg){width=165}

![](img/CONAE.png){width=165}

</center>

:::

<center><span style='font-size: 7pt;'>*No generative AI was used to make this presentation*</span></center>
